title: Being Thin Doesn't Mean Your Metabolism Is Fine
description: Roughly one in five normal-weight American adults has prediabetes — and most have no idea, because their doctors aren't looking. This piece examines the science of 'normal weight obesity': how visceral fat and low muscle mass drive insulin resistance in lean people, why BMI became medicine's laziest screening tool, and what markers actually tell you something useful about metabolic risk.
